body:
This PRD was ratified in the 2026-07-16 design session and confirmed at the subsequent `/to-prd` testing-seam checkpoint. It is the second, independently implementable entry in a two-PRD program; the first entry enables user-supplied candidate intake. The reported Segment Reconciliation truncation was refreshed against the current compiler family and expanded into the ratified prompt-wide invariant below. The active authorities are `docs/FOUNDATIONS.md`, `docs/compiler-contract.md`, `docs/story-record-schema.md`, `docs/segment-reconciliation-prompt-template.md`, `docs/story-record-hygiene-prompt-template.md`, and `docs/robustness-testing.md`.

## Problem Statement

Prompt-facing record labels can inherit the stored 80-character browse label instead of the complete label derived from the record payload. Segment Reconciliation visibly serializes values such as `display_label: ...` with an ellipsis, causing the model to receive incomplete record identity. The same stored-label leakage exists in Reconciliation reference stubs, Record Hygiene record/reference surfaces, and a prose ordering fallback.

The defect is broader than cosmetic preview text. Truncated labels can make two distinct records look identical, can change deterministic ordering when long labels share a prefix, and can weaken reference clarity for the external model. Raising the truncation limit would leave the same category of bug in place.

The repository already distinguishes editor/browse labels from full payload-derived labels. The prompt family does not consistently enforce that distinction at every serialization and ordering boundary.

## Solution

Establish one compiler-family invariant:

> Every model-facing label, reference label, citation ordering input, and compiler label tie-break derives from the complete record payload. Stored browse labels are UI-only and never influence prompt text or prompt ordering.

Apply the invariant to Segment Reconciliation full records and reference stubs, Record Hygiene records and reference summaries, prose ordering, and the existing Ideate full-label path. Remove redundant prompt serialization where necessary so one complete label is authoritative. Preserve deterministic ordering, citation keys, escaping, scope boundaries, and the rule that ENTITY and CAST MEMBER payloads remain excluded when only a reference label is allowed.

Update the compiler, schema, assistance-prompt authorities, relevant contract versions, and golden baselines in the same revision.

## User Stories

1. As an author inspecting Segment Reconciliation, I want every record label shown in full, so that I can verify exactly what the model will receive
2. As an author, I want long belief, fact, event, and pressure labels preserved without ellipses, so that nuanced record identity is not flattened
3. As an author, I want out-of-scope reference stubs to use complete labels, so that a reference remains understandable without importing the target payload
4. As an author using Record Hygiene, I want each record's authoritative prompt label to be complete, so that overlapping records can be compared accurately
5. As an author using Record Hygiene, I want outgoing and incoming reference summaries to use complete labels, so that related records are not confused by a shared truncated prefix
6. As an author, I want ENTITY and CAST MEMBER reference labels to be complete without exposing their excluded payloads, so that privacy and source boundaries remain unchanged
7. As an author, I want prose compiler ordering to use complete labels, so that long labels sharing an 80-character prefix do not fall through to record-id order
8. As an author, I want Ideate citation ordering to retain its existing complete-label behavior, so that the prompt family follows one invariant
9. As an author, I want identical inputs to retain identical prompt bytes and citation ordering, so that deterministic compilation remains intact
10. As an author, I want prompt inspection to contain no truncated browse-label variant, so that visible prompt text is the exact model-facing text
11. As an author, I want full labels preserved even when they make prompts longer, so that the compiler never silently compresses declared source
12. As an author, I want an oversize prompt to fail or warn under existing policy rather than shorten labels, so that context-budget pressure does not corrupt source identity
13. As an implementer, I want one full-label derivation rule shared across compiler surfaces, so that each prompt class does not invent its own label semantics
14. As an implementer, I want golden tests with deliberately conflicting browse and full labels, so that regression coverage proves which source wins
15. As an implementer, I want cross-pillar tests for Prose, Ideate, Hygiene, and Reconciliation, so that future compiler work cannot reintroduce stored-label leakage
16. As a maintainer, I want compiler, schema, prompt-template, and version authorities updated together, so that documentation and runtime behavior cannot drift
17. As a maintainer, I want changed compiler source to follow the current coverage and mutation policy, so that the fix is tested at the repository's assurance boundary
18. As an author, I want browse labels to remain concise in record-list UI, so that fixing prompts does not degrade ordinary navigation

## Implementation Decisions

1. **Prompt-facing full-label invariant.** Any label rendered into model-facing text, a reference summary, a citation-key ordering input, or a deterministic label tie-break is derived from the complete record payload through the canonical full-label derivation behavior.

2. **Browse labels remain UI-only.** The stored 80-character display label remains available for record lists and other explicitly browse-oriented UI. Increasing its limit or removing browse truncation is not the fix.

3. **Segment Reconciliation full records use full labels.** Full record serialization must not emit the truncated stored label. If both `display_label` and another full-label field would carry the same concept, serialize one authoritative complete label rather than preserving contradictory duplicates.

4. **Segment Reconciliation reference stubs use full labels.** Minimal stubs remain id/type/label only and remain non-targets. The snapshot builder derives the target's full label without importing the out-of-scope payload into the prompt.

5. **Record Hygiene uses one authoritative complete label.** Record blocks must not expose a truncated label beside a full label. Ordering, citation keys, record display, and the record's own model-facing label use the complete derived value.

6. **Record Hygiene reference summaries use full labels.** Incoming and outgoing reference summaries derive full labels for every referenced record. ENTITY and CAST MEMBER payloads remain excluded; deriving their label does not authorize serializing any other payload field.

7. **Prose ordering uses full labels.** Deterministic record ordering and any label-based fallback compare complete payload-derived labels before record id. Two labels with the same stored prefix but different suffixes must order by their full values rather than by id.

8. **Ideate remains on the invariant.** Existing Ideate full-label citation ordering is preserved and participates in cross-pillar regression coverage. This PRD does not change Ideate's source profile or citation-key contract.

9. **No hidden compression.** Prompt length, context-budget pressure, or UI convenience must never substitute stored browse labels for full labels. Existing oversize and warning behavior remains responsible for prompt-size handling.

10. **Determinism and escaping remain unchanged.** The fix must preserve canonical payload JSON, XML/data escaping, type ordering, record-id final tie-breaks, citation-key shapes, source counts, and prompt fingerprints for identical corrected inputs.

11. **Assistance source boundaries remain unchanged.** Reconciliation stubs remain minimal and non-targetable. Hygiene continues to exclude ENTITY and CAST MEMBER payloads. No accepted-prose, candidate, note, hidden state, or out-of-scope payload source is added.

12. **Authorities change in lockstep.** Update `docs/compiler-contract.md`, `docs/story-record-schema.md`, `docs/segment-reconciliation-prompt-template.md`, and `docs/story-record-hygiene-prompt-template.md` wherever they currently permit stored labels or describe ordering/serialization.

13. **Version and golden discipline applies.** Apply the relevant compiler/template/contract version changes required by the active change-control rules and refresh affected golden fixtures only after the full-label invariant is asserted directly.

14. **No compatibility alias.** Do not preserve parallel `truncated_label` and `full_label` prompt fields or a fallback chain that can reintroduce stored labels. Prompt consumers receive one current complete-label contract.

### Browser-visible guidance checklist mapping

| Checklist item | PRD home |
|---|---|
| package source cited | N/A - no external methodology package governs this compiler defect; the provenance preamble and Principles cite the active repository authorities |
| decision-point contract named | Implementation Decisions names full-record serialization, reference-stub serialization, reference-summary serialization, citation ordering, and prose ordering boundaries |
| required, optional, skippable, and severity-dependent fields visible | N/A - this PRD changes deterministic label serialization and ordering, not an author-entered instrument with required, optional, or skippable fields |
| doctrine at the actual decision point | Solution and Implementation Decisions place the full-label rule at each compiler serialization and ordering boundary |
| prompt packet preview, source manifest, and cold external LLM test | Testing Decisions covers prompt-preview bytes and source-boundary goldens; N/A - provider behavior and cold external LLM interpretation do not change |
| advisory/canon separation visible | Principles and Implementation Decisions preserve Reconciliation and Hygiene as advisory prompt surfaces with unchanged non-canon authority |
| skip path and reason storage | N/A - this deterministic compiler fix has no user-declinable instrument or stored skip reason |
| blockers/substance validation | Testing Decisions covers complete-label assertions, truncated-form exclusion, deterministic ordering, escaping, source boundaries, coverage, and mutation policy |
| current, next, and resume state | N/A - no browser workflow state or resume transition changes; prompt inspection displays corrected bytes through the existing lifecycle |
| read-side audit or provenance link | Problem Statement, User Stories, and Testing Decisions use prompt inspection and golden readback as the audit surface |
| cognitive walkthrough scenario | Testing Decisions defines inspection of a prompt containing two long same-prefix labels and verification of complete text and deterministic order |

## Testing Decisions

Seam confirmation: answered on 2026-07-16 with the existing snapshot-builder, compiler-golden, cross-pillar, coverage, and changed-source mutation seams. No browser smoke or new compiler interface is required because the behavior is deterministic prompt serialization.

1. **Snapshot-builder seam.** Construct records whose stored browse label is deliberately truncated while the payload derives a longer complete label. Cover Reconciliation full records, Reconciliation out-of-scope stubs, Hygiene full records, Hygiene outgoing references, and Hygiene incoming references.

2. **Reconciliation compiler golden seam.** Assert that complete labels appear in record and stub serialization, stored truncated variants do not appear, reference targets remain non-targetable, payload boundaries remain unchanged, and prompt metadata/fingerprints remain deterministic.

3. **Hygiene compiler golden seam.** Assert one complete authoritative label per record, complete labels in reference summaries, unchanged citation and source counts, continued ENTITY/CAST MEMBER payload exclusion, and no truncated stored variant in model-facing text.

4. **Prose ordering seam.** Use at least two records with identical first 80 characters, different full-label suffixes, and ids arranged opposite to label order. Assert that prompt ordering follows complete labels and uses id only after full-label equality.

5. **Ideate and cross-pillar seam.** Preserve existing Ideate full-label citation behavior and add or extend one cross-pillar matrix proving Prose, Ideate, Hygiene, and Reconciliation all ignore conflicting stored browse labels for model-facing text and ordering.

6. **Escaping and determinism seam.** Include long labels containing `<`, `>`, `&`, Unicode, and punctuation. Assert existing escaping rules, citation-key shapes, stable source order, and identical outputs for identical inputs.

7. **Changed-source assurance.** Run scoped core coverage. Apply the changed-source mutation policy to every changed locked-pillar source; the Segment Reconciliation pillar remains governed by the documented advisory/deferred pre-activation behavior in `docs/robustness-testing.md`.

8. **Canonical gates.** Run `npm run lint`, `npm run typecheck`, `npm test`, and `npm run build`.

9. **No browser smoke.** Browser navigation and interaction do not change. Component or browser tests may retain ordinary prompt-inspector rendering coverage, but the acceptance proof belongs at the deterministic snapshot/compiler boundaries.

## Principles

- `docs/FOUNDATIONS.md` §§8, 9.1, 22, 28.2, and 29 require deterministic, inspectable, complete declared prompt source with no hidden compression or token-budget eviction. This PRD strengthens those rules and requires no constitutional amendment.
- `docs/compiler-contract.md` is the domain authority for prompt sources, ordering, serialization, and change control. Its stored-label wording must be corrected in the same revision.
- `docs/story-record-schema.md` must describe complete model-facing labels while preserving record-scope and payload-exclusion boundaries.
- `docs/segment-reconciliation-prompt-template.md` must retain complete records, minimal non-targetable stubs, and no silent truncation.
- `docs/story-record-hygiene-prompt-template.md` must retain complete in-scope records and reference summaries without exposing excluded ENTITY or CAST MEMBER payloads.
- `docs/robustness-testing.md` governs scoped coverage and changed-source mutation evidence; it does not redefine product behavior.
- No deliberate exception is taken from deterministic compilation, assistance quarantine, accepted-prose exclusion, local-first ownership, or the secret firewall.

## Out of Scope

- Combining this work with user-supplied candidate intake
- Increasing or removing the UI browse-label truncation limit
- Changing record-list, search-result, or editor browse-label presentation
- Adding new prompt sources, record scopes, or accepted-prose access
- Exposing ENTITY or CAST MEMBER payloads in Record Hygiene
- Making Reconciliation reference stubs proposal targets
- Changing citation-key formats or record-type ordering
- Adding semantic ranking, similarity retrieval, summarization, or token-budget eviction
- Changing provider transport, response parsing, or OpenRouter behavior
- Adding a browser-test framework or requiring an external LLM call
- Preserving a prompt compatibility alias for truncated labels

## Further Notes

Seam confirmation was explicitly answered with the listed existing compiler-family seams.

This PRD is second in the publication program because candidate intake is the immediate trial-run blocker. The two PRDs are technically independent and may be implemented separately.

The confirmed defects are Reconciliation record/stub serialization, Hygiene record/reference serialization, and prose ordering. Ideate is included as regression-protected prior art rather than asserted as a current visible defect.

No product, mechanism, testing seam, or label decision remains open to veto. Publication as `bug` plus `ready-for-agent` is appropriate.

labels.name: needs-triage
labels.description: Maintainer needs to evaluate this issue

title: PRD: Prompt-Facing Full Labels — Eliminate Browse-Label Truncation Across Compiler Surfaces
